name: NALCAB - National Association For Latino Community Asset Builders
description_short: NALCAB is a CDFI intermediary supporting 200+ nonprofit member organizations across all 50 states focused on Latino community economic development through lending, capacity building, and policy advocacy.
description_long:
NALCAB (National Association for Latino Community Asset Builders) was established as a one-of-a-kind CDFI intermediary organization dedicated to maximizing the economic contributions of the Latino population to the U.S. economy. The organization operates a network of 200+ member CDFI and economic development nonprofit organizations with presence spanning all 50 states, Washington DC, and Puerto Rico.

NALCAB provides four primary areas of support to member organizations: Capital (lending, loan guarantees, grants, and impact investing opportunities), Capacity (technical assistance, webinars, workshops, and tailored training), Collective Voice (policy advocacy at local, state, and federal levels), and Community (peer-to-peer connections, national conferences, and leadership development). In 2024 alone, NALCAB distributed 84 grants totaling $2,228,500 to community economic development nonprofits and trained 1,052 professionals. The organization has surpassed $50 million in lending volume and provides advisory services to government agencies and congressional offices.

What distinguishes NALCAB is its intermediary model—it doesn't directly serve individual consumers but rather supports and strengthens the nonprofit organizations that do. The organization operates as both a funder and capacity builder, offering individualized technical assistance alongside capital access. Their work spans multiple sectors including neighborhood development, small business development, financial capability, and housing (evidenced by their $650,000 loan to Centro para la Reconstrucción del Hábitat for Puerto Rico housing crisis response).

NALCAB functions as a free-help resource for nonprofit organizations and their affiliated communities. While not a direct consumer credit counseling service, it serves the broader ecosystem of organizations providing financial assistance and community development. End consumers typically access NALCAB's impact indirectly through their member organization networks rather than through direct service.

pricing.guarantee_details: Contact provider for current pricing and guarantee details.

pros: Operates a nationwide network of 200+ member CDFIs and nonprofits across all 50 states, DC, and Puerto Rico
Distributed $2,228,500 in grants to 84 nonprofit organizations in 2024 alone
Provides both capital access (loans, guarantees, grants, impact investing) and capacity building (training, technical assistance, webinars)
Trained 1,052 community economic development professionals in 2024
Engages in policy advocacy at local, state, and federal levels on behalf of member organizations and communities
Offers specialized programs like Maker to Market Accelerator and Cafecito regional convenings
Has surpassed $50 million in cumulative lending volume to support community economic development
cons: Not a direct consumer service—individuals cannot access NALCAB services directly; must go through member organizations
Limited transparency on specific eligibility criteria and application processes for grants and lending programs
Website does not provide contact information for direct inquiries or a clear path for prospective member organizations seeking to join
Focused exclusively on Latino community economic development, which while intentional, limits broader accessibility
No individual financial counseling, credit repair, or direct consumer financial services offered
best_for: Nonprofit organizations and CDFIs working on community economic development seeking grants, loans, and capacity building support
Communities in Latino-serving regions seeking housing, small business, or neighborhood development initiatives
Policymakers and government agencies seeking advisory services on community development and Latino economic issues
Economic development professionals seeking training, networking, and peer learning opportunities
diagnosis: NALCAB is best for nonprofit organizations, CDFIs, and community development professionals seeking to strengthen Latino community economic development through grants, capital access, and capacity building. The main caveat is that NALCAB is an intermediary organization—individual consumers cannot directly apply for or receive services; they must access support through member organizations in their community.
services: Lending and loan guarantees to member CDFI organizations
Grant funding for community economic development nonprofits
Individualized technical assistance for member organizations
Webinars and workshops on community development topics
Tailored training and leadership development programs
Policy advocacy and advisory services to government agencies
National Conference hosting (2026 conference scheduled San Antonio, September 15-17)
Regional Cafecito convenings for peer-to-peer networking
typical_results_timeline: Results vary by individual situation. Contact the provider to discuss expected timelines for your specific needs.
